zh.lifeSummary: 當Basilia de Clare於大約1156年出生在英國英格蘭肯特托布利其時，她的父親Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke27歲，母親Unknown mistress25歲。她於1174年與Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor在英國英格蘭結婚。她去世於大約1203年在英國英格蘭享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Basilia de Clare于大约1156年出生在英国英格兰肯特托布利其时，她的父亲Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke27岁，母亲Unknown mistress25岁。她于1174年与Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor在英国英格兰结婚。她去世于大约1203年在英国英格兰享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Basilia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Domingo, Roberto, Carlos, Ricardo, Albertino, Alejandro, Almario, Altagracia, Andres, Armando.  Portuguese Ilidio.  Italian Antonio, Gennaro, Anastasio, Federico, Marco, Quirino.
Italian; Portuguese (Basílio): from the personal name Basilio, Basílio, from Latin Basilius (see Basil ).

variants.name: Basil
meanings: From the Greek name Basileios ‘royal’ (a derivative of basileus ‘king’). This name was borne by St Basil the Great ( c.330–379 ), bishop of Caesarea, a theologian regarded as one of the Fathers of the Eastern Church. It was also the name of several early saints martyred in the East.

variants.name: Bacilio
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Peru): variant spelling of   Basilio  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

en.lifeSummary: When Basilia de Clare was born about 1156, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, was 27 and her mother, Unknown mistress, was 25. She married Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor in 1174, in England, United Kingdom. She died about 1203, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Basilia de Clare est née environ 1156, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Unknown mistress, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor en 1174, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée environ 1203, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Basilia de Clare um 1156 in Tonbridge, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke 27 und ihre Mutter Unknown mistress 25. Sie hat 1174 in England, United Kingdom Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor geheiratet. Sie starb um 1203 im Alter von 49 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Basilia de Clare è nata il circa 1156 nel Tonbridge, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard II de Clare 2nd Earl of Pembroke, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Unknown mistress, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato Raymond FitzWilliam De'Windsor nel 1174, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì circa 1203, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.
